reasoning: The Lean statement captures exactly the informal claim. The hypotheses match: `p` is prime (`hp : Nat.Prime p`), `q` is a polynomial over `ZMod p` (which is the field $\mathbb{Z}_p$ since `p` is prime), `q` is irreducible, and `q.degree = n` (the `WithBot ℕ` coercion is unambiguous since irreducible polynomials are nonzero). The conclusion has the two parts asked for: (i) the quotient `Polynomial (ZMod p) ⧸ span {q}` is finite with cardinality $p^n$ (via the existentially quantified `Fintype` instance and `Fintype.card = p^n`), and (ii) it is a field (`IsField`). Since all `Fintype` instances on a type give the same cardinality, the existential is equivalent to the quotient being finite of size $p^n$. The ideal `span {q}` here is `Ideal.span {q}` (the namespace is opened), which is the principal ideal generated by `q`, matching `(q(x))`. No extra hypotheses are assumed and none are dropped.
